description: Loop Over Items with reset:true for polling a long-running job — Wait between checks, status IF with $runIndex ceiling, termination via break-out branch not natural done output.

conversation.text: On form submit, start a background export by POSTing to https://api.example.com/exports, then poll GET https://api.example.com/exports/{job_id}/status every 2 seconds — include an incrementing attempt query parameter on each check (1, 2, …) — until status is complete or you've checked 10 times, whichever comes first. Then grab the download URL from the result.

outcomeExpectations: After starting the export, the workflow polls job status in a loop (Loop Over Items with reset or equivalent) with a delay between checks (around 2 seconds).
Polling terminates when status is complete OR an iteration cap of 10 is reached — explicit stop condition, not an unbounded loop.
The final step reads the download URL from the completed job response.

executionScenarios.name: completes-on-second-poll
executionScenarios.description: Job pending then complete; download URL extracted
executionScenarios.dataSetup: POST https://api.example.com/exports returns { "job_id": "exp-42" }. Status polling uses GET https://api.example.com/exports/exp-42/status with an incrementing attempt query parameter — identical request URLs are cached in mock execution, so each poll MUST use a distinct attempt value. GET .../status?attempt=1 returns { "status": "pending", "poll_count": 1 }. GET .../status?attempt=2 (and any attempt>=2) returns { "status": "complete", "download_url": "https://cdn.example.com/files/report.csv", "poll_count": 2 }. Never return identical JSON for different attempt values or n8n stops with 'identical response 5x'.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. At least two status GET requests occur (attempt=1 then attempt=2 or higher) before succeeding. The workflow output or final step references the download URL https://cdn.example.com/files/report.csv (or the path report.csv). Polling stops after completion — it does not continue indefinitely.
